prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_component_instance local_execution_context_1)
(acquire_shared_resource local_execution_context_1 shared_resource_lock_a)
(bind_input_event local_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(finalize_event_binding local_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(initiate_local_ordering_token local_execution_context_1 ordering_token_1 input_event_1)
(attach_service_dependency local_execution_context_1 service_dependency_api)
(confirm_local_ordering_token local_execution_context_1 ordering_token_1 service_dependency_api)
(release_input_event local_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(register_component_instance concurrent_execution_context_1)
(acquire_shared_resource concurrent_execution_context_1 shared_resource_lock_b)
(bind_input_event concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(finalize_event_binding concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(initiate_concurrent_ordering_token concurrent_execution_context_1 condition_flag_1 input_event_1)
(claim_concurrent_ordering_token_with_signal concurrent_execution_context_1 condition_flag_1 external_signal_1)
(reserve_and_bind_test_scenario_branch_b local_execution_context_1 concurrent_execution_context_1 ordering_token_1 condition_flag_1 test_scenario_repro_1)
(release_input_event concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(bind_input_event local_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(execute_test_scenario test_scenario_repro_1 local_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(mark_local_context_integration_ready local_execution_context_1 test_scenario_repro_1)
(release_input_event local_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(bind_input_event concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(propagate_concurrent_ordering_token concurrent_execution_context_1 condition_flag_1 input_event_1 external_signal_1)
(mark_concurrent_context_integration_ready concurrent_execution_context_1 test_scenario_repro_1)
(release_input_event concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(register_component_instance candidate_alpha)
(acquire_shared_resource candidate_alpha shared_resource_lock_c)
(bind_input_event candidate_alpha input_event_1)
(finalize_event_binding candidate_alpha input_event_1)
(attach_timing_spec_to_candidate candidate_alpha timing_spec_short_timeout)
(detach_service_dependency local_execution_context_1 service_dependency_api)
(attach_service_dependency candidate_alpha service_dependency_api)
(stage_configuration_option candidate_alpha config_option_feature_x test_scenario_repro_1)
(apply_config_option_to_candidate candidate_alpha config_option_feature_x test_scenario_repro_1 input_event_1)
(attach_timing_spec_with_dependency_branch candidate_alpha timing_spec_short_timeout service_dependency_api config_option_feature_x test_scenario_repro_1)
(attach_reviewer_approval_to_candidate candidate_alpha reviewer_approval_lead)
(apply_reviewer_approval_and_confirm_config_alt candidate_alpha reviewer_approval_lead config_option_feature_x test_scenario_repro_1)
(attach_invariant_to_candidate candidate_alpha invariant_non_null)
(lock_candidate_for_integration candidate_alpha local_execution_context_1 concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1 invariant_non_null)
(finalize_candidate_integration candidate_alpha)
(apply_patch_to_component candidate_alpha env_param_ci_mode input_event_1)
(apply_candidate_patch_and_release_resource candidate_alpha shared_resource_lock_c env_param_ci_mode)
(release_input_event candidate_alpha input_event_1)
(bind_input_event local_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(apply_patch_to_component local_execution_context_1 env_param_ci_mode input_event_1)
(apply_patch_and_release_resource_local local_execution_context_1 shared_resource_lock_a env_param_ci_mode)
(release_input_event local_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(bind_input_event concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(apply_patch_to_component concurrent_execution_context_1 env_param_ci_mode input_event_1)
(apply_patch_and_release_resource_concurrent concurrent_execution_context_1 shared_resource_lock_b env_param_ci_mode)
(release_input_event concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1)
(register_component_instance candidate_beta)
(detach_timing_spec_from_candidate candidate_alpha timing_spec_short_timeout)
(detach_reviewer_approval_from_candidate candidate_alpha reviewer_approval_lead)
(detach_service_dependency candidate_alpha service_dependency_api)
(acquire_shared_resource candidate_beta shared_resource_lock_a)
(bind_input_event candidate_beta input_event_1)
(finalize_event_binding candidate_beta input_event_1)
(attach_timing_spec_to_candidate candidate_beta timing_spec_short_timeout)
(attach_service_dependency candidate_beta service_dependency_api)
(attach_reviewer_approval_to_candidate candidate_beta reviewer_approval_lead)
(stage_configuration_option candidate_beta config_option_feature_y test_scenario_repro_1)
(apply_config_option_to_candidate candidate_beta config_option_feature_y test_scenario_repro_1 input_event_1)
(attach_timing_spec_with_dependency_branch candidate_beta timing_spec_short_timeout service_dependency_api config_option_feature_y test_scenario_repro_1)
(apply_reviewer_approval_and_confirm_config_alt candidate_beta reviewer_approval_lead config_option_feature_y test_scenario_repro_1)
(attach_invariant_to_candidate candidate_beta invariant_ordering_constraint)
(lock_candidate_for_integration candidate_beta local_execution_context_1 concurrent_execution_context_1 input_event_1 invariant_ordering_constraint)
(finalize_candidate_integration candidate_beta)
(apply_patch_to_component candidate_beta env_param_ci_mode input_event_1)
(apply_candidate_patch_and_release_resource candidate_beta shared_resource_lock_a env_param_ci_mode)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a complex planning domain with many predicates and actions for managing dependencies, contexts, candidates, and resources. The goal is to commit fixes for local_execution_context_1, concurrent_execution_context_1, candidate_alpha, and candidate_beta.


## Investigating Problem Details
- The task involves a complex planning domain with multiple elements like dependencies, contexts, and resources.
- The goal focuses on committing fixes for specific contexts (local_execution_context_1, concurrent_execution_context_1) and candidates (candidate_alpha, candidate_beta).

## Examining Registration Process
- All component instances, including local_execution_context_1, concurrent_execution_context_1, candidate_alpha, and candidate_beta, must be registered first.
- The registration process starts with the action "register_component_instance" for each component.

## Assessing Resource Needs
- Components require shared resources like shared_resource_lock_a, b, and c, which they must acquire.
- Only one input_event_1 is available, suggesting a need to sequence binding and releasing due to shared usage.

## Exploring Integration Paths
- To achieve fix_committed, components must first reach ready_for_integration through various actions like mark_local_context_integration_ready or finalize_candidate.
- Different paths exist, such as applying patches with env_param and releasing resources, tailored to contexts (local/concurrent) and candidates.

## Exploring Event Propagation  
- Event propagation requires entity_initialized, context_token_binding, external_signal_available, and not local_context_ready, setting claimed, ready, local_signal_binding, and not signal_available.  
- Further propagation needs instance_event_binding, claimed, local_signal_binding, and not confirmed, setting asserted, confirmed, signal_available, and not binding.  

## Analyzing Test Scenario Reservation  
- Reserve_and_bind_test_scenario requires both asserted and confirmed; variants include branch_a (token claimed, flag asserted, local not confirmed, concurrent confirmed) and branch_b (token asserted, flag claimed, local confirmed, concurrent not).  
- Both_branches variant needs both claimed and both not confirmed, with execute_test_scenario requiring reserved, local_context_ready, instance_event_binding, and not completed, setting completed.  

## Detailing Candidate Integration Paths  
- Candidates need entity_initialized and follow paths like candidate_finalized to reach ready_for_integration, potentially involving config staging and source binding.  
- Stage_configuration_option requires entity_initialized, candidate_includes_test, and other conditions, setting staged and applied_to_scenario, while apply_config_option_to_candidate sets option_ready if certain conditions are met.

## Examining Event Management  
- After confirming, the input event can be released, making it available again for other contexts, such as local_execution_context_1 releasing input_event_1.  
- Concurrent execution contexts can then bind and initialize with the same input event, like concurrent_execution_context_1 binding to input_event_1.  

## Sequencing Dependency Handling  
- Service dependencies, initially bound to local contexts, may need to be detached after confirmation to allow use by concurrent contexts, such as detaching service_dependency_api from local.  
- Concurrent contexts might use alternative paths like claiming signals instead of dependencies, as seen with claim_concurrent_ordering_token_with_signal for concurrent_execution_context_1.  

## Analyzing Test Scenario Reservation  
- Standard reserve_and_bind_test_scenario requires both local and concurrent contexts to be confirmed, which may not align if concurrent is ready but not confirmed.  
- Branch_b variant fits better when local is confirmed, concurrent is ready but not confirmed, and flag is claimed, matching the sequence observed.

## Extending Execution Steps  
- After reserving, release and rebind input_event_1 to manage context transitions, starting with concurrent_execution_context_1 releasing it.  
- Local_execution_context_1 then binds input_event_1, executes test_scenario_repro_1, and marks itself ready for integration.  

## Handling Concurrent Context Confirmation  
- Concurrent context is ready but needs confirmation; rebind input_event_1 to concurrent_execution_context_1 after releasing from local.  
- Propagate_concurrent_ordering_token then confirms concurrent context, setting it ready_for_integration alongside local.  

## Managing Resource and Candidate Initialization  
- Register and acquire shared resources for candidates like candidate_alpha, ensuring input_event_1 is available by releasing it from concurrent.  
- Bind input_event_1 to candidate_alpha and finalize the event binding to initialize, preparing for further candidate setup.

## Staging Local Context  
- Input_event_1, bound to candidate_alpha, needs releasing to stage local_execution_context_1; release via candidate_alpha.  
- Local_execution_context_1 then binds input_event_1, applying env_param_ci_mode patch, setting patch_staged and instance_env_binding.  

## Committing Local Resources  
- Apply patch and release shared_resource_lock_a for local_execution_context_1, marking it committed and freeing resources.  
- Input_event_1 remains bound to local_execution_context_1 post-commit, requiring release for other contexts.  

## Managing Concurrent Context  
- Release input_event_1 from local_execution_context_1, then bind to concurrent_execution_context_1 for staging.  
- Apply env_param_ci_mode patch for concurrent, commit using shared_resource_lock_b, freeing resources and completing staging.  

## Finalizing Candidate Setup  
- With test scenario reserved, candidate_alpha stages configuration_option_feature_x, applying it to test_scenario_repro_1.  
- Next steps involve applying config options, but require re-binding input_event_1, currently unbound after releases.

## Exploring Candidate Paths  
- Alpha's path didn't rely on source_bound or mocks, using config options and timing specs to reach readiness.  
- Beta faces issues sharing timing_spec and reviewer, as both are single-use and candidate-specific.

## Addressing Resource Constraints  
- Timing_spec and reviewer are limited to one each, requiring detachment and reattachment between candidates.  
- Mock_service_b isn't available for beta, limiting mock-based paths and suggesting alternative strategies without source_bound.

## Reevaluating Validation Process  
- Candidate validation requires reviewer actions, all of which need timing binding and reviewer approval, limiting concurrent use.  
- Detaching and reattaching resources like timing and reviewer could allow sequential validation for both alpha and beta.

## Refining Sequence Details  
- The sequence starts with registering local_execution_context_1, acquiring shared_resource_lock_a, and binding input_event_1 to it.  
- After confirming the local ordering token and service dependency, input_event_1 is released to make it available for concurrent_execution_context_1.  

## Correcting Binding Errors  
- Initial attempt to bind input_event_1 to concurrent_execution_context_1 failed as it was still bound to local; release is necessary before binding.  
- Full sequence reordered to ensure input_event_1 availability, fixing earlier mistakes in binding steps.  

## Streamlining Candidate Setup  
- Alpha's reserve_source step at 44 was unnecessary and can be omitted, simplifying the sequence.  
- Beta doesn't require source, and service dependency detachment aligns with resource availability after context commits.  

## Reorganizing Execution Flow  
- Contexts must be committed before alpha and beta initialization to ensure resource availability.  
- Detaching service dependency from local after commit supports alpha's later attachment, maintaining the correct order.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
